milestones.title: M001: Migration
milestones.vision: The LSP Indexer is an open-source blockchain event indexer for the LUKSO network.

milestones.title: M002: Docs Site & AI Agent Compatibility
milestones.vision: Migrate `apps/test` to `apps/docs` — the public-facing developer hub for `@lsp-indexer`. The app already has 12 domain playground pages, a custom docs section, and a Dockerfile deployment setup. This milestone renames it, upgrades the docs section to Fumadocs (proper sidebar, search, syntax highlighting), and adds AI-compatibility artifacts (llms.txt, llms-full.txt, per-page .md sidecars, context7.json) so LLM tools and MCP clients can consume the documentation programmatically. The playground stays intact and coexists with the improved docs section.
success_criteria: `apps/test` is renamed to `apps/docs` with package name `docs` — all references updated (pnpm-workspace.yaml, Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, CI)
The 12 domain playground pages are fully functional in `apps/docs` (all hooks, filters, sorts, includes work as before)
The docs section is powered by Fumadocs: sidebar navigation, full-text search, syntax highlighting, dark mode — replacing the current custom `@next/mdx` + prose layout
All 5 docs pages (Quickstart, Indexer, Node, React, Next) render correctly under Fumadocs
`GET /llms.txt` returns a concise project summary for LLMs
`GET /llms-full.txt` returns all 5 docs pages concatenated
`GET /llm/[slug].md` returns raw markdown for each of the 5 pages (collision-free path, outside Fumadocs routes)
`context7.json` exists at `public/context7.json` formatted for Context7 MCP indexing
`pnpm --filter docs generate:check` exits 0 in clean state and non-zero when any sidecar drifts from its MDX source
`pnpm --filter docs build` exits 0; `apps/test` is removed
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
## Boundary Map

### S01 → S02

Produces:
- `apps/docs/` — complete working app with all 12 playground pages and current docs layout
- `apps/docs/package.json` — name: `docs`
- `apps/docs/Dockerfile`, `apps/docs/docker-compose.yml` — updated paths
- All CI references updated to `apps/docs`
- `pnpm-workspace.yaml` — still uses `apps/*` glob (no change needed, but verified)

Consumes:
- nothing (first slice — pure rename)

### S02 → S03

Produces:
- `apps/docs/app/docs/layout.tsx` — replaced with Fumadocs `DocsLayout`
- `apps/docs/app/docs/[[...slug]]/page.tsx` — Fumadocs catch-all renderer (replaces 5 individual `page.mdx` dirs)
- `apps/docs/content/docs/` — MDX files with Fumadocs frontmatter (migrated from `app/docs/*/page.mdx`)
- `apps/docs/source.config.ts` — Fumadocs content source
- Working Fumadocs search at `/docs/*`
- `pnpm --filter docs build` exits 0

Consumes from S01:
- `apps/docs/` base app — providers.tsx, root layout, nav sidebar for playground routes

### S03 → S04

Produces:
- `apps/docs/app/llms.txt/route.ts` — GET handler returning project summary; per-page links reference `/llm/{slug}.md`
- `apps/docs/app/llms-full.txt/route.ts` — GET handler reading from `public/llm/` and concatenating
- `apps/docs/scripts/generate-md.mjs` — default: writes `public/llm/{slug}.md`; `--check`: exits 1 if any file is stale or missing
- `apps/docs/public/llm/*.md` — 5 static files committed to repo, served by Next.js at `/llm/{slug}.md`
- `"generate"` and `"generate:check"` scripts in `apps/docs/package.json`
- Verified: `GET /llms.txt`, `GET /llms-full.txt`, `GET /llm/quickstart.md` return expected content

Consumes from S02:
- `apps/docs/content/docs/` MDX files — generate script reads these as source

### S04 → (done)

Produces:
- `apps/docs/public/context7.json` — Context7 MCP library index
- CI job/step running `pnpm --filter docs generate:check` on every PR
- Verified: stale sidecar causes CI job to fail with slug name in output

Consumes from S03:
- `apps/docs/scripts/generate-md.mjs` with `--check` flag
- `public/llm/*.md` static files (committed, checked in CI)

milestones.title: M003: useIsFollowingBatch Hook
milestones.vision: Add a `useIsFollowingBatch` hook across all packages (`@lsp-indexer/types`, `@lsp-indexer/node`, `@lsp-indexer/react`, `@lsp-indexer/next`) to batch-check whether multiple address pairs have follow relationships, plus a playground component.
success_criteria: [x] `UseIsFollowingBatchParamsSchema` Zod schema and types in `@lsp-indexer/types`
[x] `fetchIsFollowingBatch` with `_or` Hasura query in `@lsp-indexer/node`
[x] `followerKeys.isFollowingBatch(pairs)` cache key factory entry
[x] `createUseIsFollowingBatch` factory + concrete hook in `@lsp-indexer/react`
[x] `getIsFollowingBatch` server action with Map→Record serialization in `@lsp-indexer/next`
[x] `useIsFollowingBatch` hook with Map reconstruction in `@lsp-indexer/next`
[x] `BatchIsFollowingTab` playground component on `/follows` page
[x] React and Next.js docs updated with `useIsFollowingBatch` sections
[x] Case-insensitive address normalization for checksum vs lowercase mismatches

milestones.title: M004: Mutual Follow Hooks
milestones.vision: Three social graph intersection hooks — `useMutualFollows`, `useMutualFollowers`, and `useFollowedByMyFollows` — exposing mutual connection queries through the typed consumer package layer.
success_criteria: `useMutualFollows(addressA, addressB)` returns profiles both A and B follow
`useMutualFollowers(addressA, addressB)` returns profiles that follow both A and B
`useFollowedByMyFollows(myAddress, targetAddress)` returns profiles user follows who also follow target
All three have infinite scroll variants
Include-based type narrowing works on returned profiles
All 4 packages build and typecheck clean
Hooks available from both `@lsp-indexer/react` and `@lsp-indexer/next`
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
## Boundary Map

### S01 → S02

Produces:
- `packages/types/src/followers.ts` → `UseMutualFollowsParamsSchema`, `UseMutualFollowersParamsSchema`, `UseFollowedByMyFollowsParamsSchema` + inferred types
- `packages/node/src/documents/followers.ts` → `GetMutualFollowsDocument` (or shared profile document with mutual follow where-clause)
- `packages/node/src/services/followers.ts` → `fetchMutualFollows()`, `fetchMutualFollowers()`, `fetchFollowedByMyFollows()`
- `packages/node/src/keys/followers.ts` → `followerKeys.mutualFollows()`, `.mutualFollowers()`, `.followedByMyFollows()`
- `packages/react/src/hooks/followers/` → `useMutualFollows`, `useMutualFollowers`, `useFollowedByMyFollows` + infinite variants
- `packages/next/src/hooks/followers/` → same hooks via server actions
- `packages/next/src/actions/followers.ts` → `getMutualFollows`, `getMutualFollowers`, `getFollowedByMyFollows` server actions

Consumes:
- nothing (first slice)

### S02

Produces:
- `apps/test/src/app/mutual-follows/page.tsx` → playground page
- `apps/docs/` → updated docs pages

Consumes from S01:
- All hook exports from react and next packages

milestones.title: M005: Batch Encrypted Asset Fetch
milestones.vision: Fetch multiple encrypted assets by `(address, contentId, revision)` tuples in a single Hasura round trip, with full include type narrowing across all four consumer packages.
success_criteria: `fetchEncryptedAssetsBatch` accepts an array of tuples and returns matching encrypted assets in one query
Address comparison uses `_ilike`, contentId and revision use `_eq`
React and Next.js hooks expose batch with full `EncryptedAssetInclude` type narrowing
Docs page documents the batch API
Changeset ready for minor release
`pnpm build` exits 0 across all 5 packages
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
## Boundary Map

### S01 → S02

Produces:
- `EncryptedAssetBatchTuple` type and `UseEncryptedAssetsBatchParams` schema in `packages/types/src/encrypted-assets.ts`
- `fetchEncryptedAssetsBatch` service function in `packages/node/src/services/encrypted-assets.ts`
- `encryptedAssetKeys.batch()` key factory entry in `packages/node/src/keys/encrypted-assets.ts`
- `createUseEncryptedAssetsBatch` factory in `packages/react/src/hooks/factories/encrypted-assets/`
- `useEncryptedAssetsBatch` concrete hook in `packages/react/src/hooks/encrypted-assets/`
- Next.js server action `encryptedAssetsBatchAction` in `packages/next/src/actions/encrypted-assets.ts`
- Next.js `useEncryptedAssetsBatch` hook in `packages/next/src/hooks/encrypted-assets/`

Consumes:
- nothing (first slice)

### S02 consumes from S01:
- All exported batch functions, hooks, and types for documentation
- Package names for changeset

milestones.vision: Fix the production crash in the indexer's VERIFY step where viem's hexToBool() throws InvalidHexBooleanError on non-boolean hex from supportsInterface. Replace all 3 call sites with a safe helper that treats invalid hex as false. Unblock the indexer from its restart loop at block 7,137,664.
success_criteria: safeHexToBool utility exists in packages/indexer/src/utils/index.ts and handles non-boolean hex without throwing
All 3 hexToBool call sites (verification.ts, orbsClaimed.handler.ts, chillClaimed.handler.ts) use safeHexToBool instead
No raw hexToBool imports remain in the indexer package
pnpm --filter=@chillwhales/indexer build passes with zero errors

key_risks.risk: Near-zero risk — targeted defensive fix to 3 files
key_risks.whyItMatters: The only subtlety is correctly identifying true from ABI-encoded boolean responses (padded 0x...0001)

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: safeHexToBool correctly returns true for 0x0...0001, false for 0x0...0000, and false for any other value — verified by build + code review

milestones.verification_contract: pnpm --filter=@chillwhales/indexer build exits 0. No raw hexToBool imports remain (rg hexToBool returns 0 hits or only the safe wrapper).
milestones.verification_integration: None — local code change within the indexer package only.
milestones.verification_operational: None — operational proof requires production deployment (out of scope).
milestones.verification_uat: Indexer builds cleanly. Code review confirms all 3 call sites use safeHexToBool.
definition_of_done: safeHexToBool utility created and used at all 3 call sites
No raw hexToBool calls remain in the indexer codebase
pnpm --filter=@chillwhales/indexer build passes with zero errors
milestones.requirement_coverage: Covers: R015, R016. Leaves for later: none. Orphan risks: none.
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
### S01 (leaf slice)

Produces:
- `packages/indexer/src/utils/index.ts` → `safeHexToBool(hex: string): boolean` utility
- Updated `verification.ts` → uses safeHexToBool in multicallVerify
- Updated `orbsClaimed.handler.ts` → uses safeHexToBool in result processing
- Updated `chillClaimed.handler.ts` → uses safeHexToBool in result processing

Consumes: nothing (single slice milestone)

slices.title: Handler Migration
slices.demo: Add infrastructure changes that all Phase 1 handlers depend on: async handler support, a delete queue for DB-level entity removal, a post-verification handler hook (Step 5.
slices.goal: Add infrastructure changes that all Phase 1 handlers depend on: async handler support, a delete queue for DB-level entity removal, a post-verification handler hook (Step 5.

slices.title: New Handlers Structured Logging
slices.demo: Build the structured logging module that provides consistent JSON log output across all pipeline steps.
slices.goal: Build the structured logging module that provides consistent JSON log output across all pipeline steps.

slices.title: Metadata Fetch Handlers
slices.demo: Build the foundation layer for all three metadata fetch handlers: extend FetchResult to preserve error details, port V1 type guards, and create the shared fetch utility.
slices.goal: Build the foundation layer for all three metadata fetch handlers: extend FetchResult to preserve error details, port V1 type guards, and create the shared fetch utility.

slices.title: Improve Debug Logging Strategy
slices.demo: Add component-specific debug logging infrastructure with configurable filtering, enabling faster debugging of worker pool and metadata fetch operations without code modifications.
slices.goal: Add component-specific debug logging infrastructure with configurable filtering, enabling faster debugging of worker pool and metadata fetch operations without code modifications.

slices.title: Integration Wiring
slices.demo: unit tests prove integration-wiring works
slices.goal: unit tests prove integration-wiring works

slices.title: Deployment Validation
slices.demo: Build the foundation layer for the V1 vs V2 comparison tool: type definitions, entity registry with all 72 Hasura entity types and known divergence exclusions, and a GraphQL client that can query both aggregate counts and paginated row samples from Hasura endpoints.
slices.goal: Build the foundation layer for the V1 vs V2 comparison tool: type definitions, entity registry with all 72 Hasura entity types and known divergence exclusions, and a GraphQL client that can query both aggregate counts and paginated row samples from Hasura endpoints.

slices.title: Pipeline Bug Fix Missing Handlers
slices.demo: Fix the contract filter address comparison bug in pipeline.
slices.goal: Fix the contract filter address comparison bug in pipeline.

slices.title: Lsp4 Base Uri Count Parity
slices.demo: Fix OwnedAsset double-processing bug and mark LSP8ReferenceContract as known V1 divergence.
slices.goal: Fix OwnedAsset double-processing bug and mark LSP8ReferenceContract as known V1 divergence.

slices.title: Entity Upsert Pattern Standardization
slices.demo: Create `resolveEntity<T>()` and `resolveEntities<T>()` helpers in handlerHelpers.
slices.goal: Create `resolveEntity<T>()` and `resolveEntities<T>()` helpers in handlerHelpers.

slices.title: Tech Debt Cleanup
slices.demo: Remove stale code artifacts and replace JSON.
slices.goal: Remove stale code artifacts and replace JSON.

slices.title: Package Foundation
slices.demo: Create the `packages/react` package with working ESM+CJS+DTS builds, GraphQL codegen pipeline, `IndexerError` class with industry-standard error taxonomy, typed GraphQL fetch wrapper, and env-based URL configuration — validated by publint and arethetypeswrong.
slices.goal: Create the `packages/react` package with working ESM+CJS+DTS builds, GraphQL codegen pipeline, `IndexerError` class with industry-standard error taxonomy, typed GraphQL fetch wrapper, and env-based URL configuration — validated by publint and arethetypeswrong.

slices.goal: Create the Profile domain types and GraphQL documents — the foundation layer that everything else builds on.

slices.demo: Create the Digital Asset domain types (Zod schemas + inferred TS types) and GraphQL documents — the foundation layer for the entire digital assets vertical slice.
slices.goal: Create the Digital Asset domain types (Zod schemas + inferred TS types) and GraphQL documents — the foundation layer for the entire digital assets vertical slice.

slices.title: Subscription Foundation
slices.demo: Complete type-safety revamp of subscription infrastructure, export where-clause builders from all 12 domain services, and wire the test app for subscription development.
slices.goal: Complete type-safety revamp of subscription infrastructure, export where-clause builders from all 12 domain services, and wire the test app for subscription development.

slices.goal: Normalize all private package versions to 0.

slices.goal: Add block ordering fields to the schema and type system foundation.

slices.goal: Establish structured logging conventions and migrate core infrastructure (registry, config, startup) from console.

slices.goal: Add performance timing to every pipeline step and emit a batch summary log at the end of processBatch().

slices.goal: Add 5 new panel groups to the Grafana dashboard that surface pipeline health, entity throughput, verification status, and metadata fetch progress using structured log data from Phases 20.

slices.title: Sorting Consumer Package Release
slices.demo: **Deferred** — VPS/volume-level snapshots cover the backup need; a pg_dump sidecar adds complexity without benefit. M001 marked complete without S30.
slices.goal: Add consistent `newest`/`oldest` block-order sorting across all 12 domain types and services.

slices.title: Rename apps/test → apps/docs
slices.demo: `apps/test` is gone, `apps/docs` exists with package name `docs`, `pnpm --filter docs build` exits 0, and the app runs identically to before at `http://localhost:3001` — all 12 playground pages functional, docs layout unchanged, Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml updated.
slices.goal: Rename the entire `apps/test` directory to `apps/docs` and update all references so the app is identical in behavior under its new name — package name `docs`, Dockerfile updated, docker-compose.yml updated, CI references updated.

slices.title: Migrate Docs Section to Fumadocs
slices.demo: The docs section at `/docs/*` is powered by Fumadocs with proper sidebar navigation, full-text search, and syntax highlighting. All 5 docs pages render correctly. The playground sidebar and playground routes continue to work alongside Fumadocs. Proven by running `pnpm --filter docs dev` and navigating both playground and docs routes in a browser.
slices.goal: Replace the current custom `@next/mdx` docs layout in `apps/docs` with Fumadocs — giving the docs section a proper sidebar, full-text search, and syntax highlighting — while keeping the existing playground routes and sidebar fully functional alongside it.

slices.title: AI Compatibility — llms.txt, llms-full.txt, .md Sidecars
slices.demo: `curl http://localhost:3001/llms.txt` returns a project summary, `curl http://localhost:3001/llms-full.txt` returns all docs concatenated, and `curl http://localhost:3001/llm/quickstart.md` returns raw markdown — all served from `public/llm/` static files (no Fumadocs route collision).
slices.goal: Add three AI-agent HTTP surfaces to `apps/docs`: `/llms.txt` (project summary for LLMs), `/llms-full.txt` (all docs concatenated), and static `/llm/{slug}.md` files served from `public/llm/` — generated by a script from the MDX sources, committed to the repo. The `/llm/` path avoids any collision with Fumadocs's `[[...slug]]` catch-all under `/docs/`.

slices.title: context7.json + CI Sidecar Validation
slices.demo: `public/context7.json` lists all 5 doc pages in Context7 format, `pnpm --filter docs generate:check` exits 0 on a clean repo and exits 1 when a `.mdx` source diverges from its sidecar output — with the check wired into CI.
slices.goal: Add `public/context7.json` for Context7 MCP library indexing, and wire the sidecar check script from S03 into CI so that PRs fail if `.md` sidecars are not committed and up to date.

slices.title: useIsFollowingBatch across all packages
slices.demo: Users can batch-check follow relationships with `useIsFollowingBatch` in React and Next.js, with a working playground demo on the `/follows` page.

slices.title: Mutual Follow Hooks — Full Stack
slices.demo: All 3 hooks return correct profile intersections from live Hasura in both `@lsp-indexer/react` and `@lsp-indexer/next`, with infinite scroll and include narrowing.
slices.goal: All 3 mutual follow hooks (`useMutualFollows`, `useMutualFollowers`, `useFollowedByMyFollows`) return correct profile intersections from live Hasura in both `@lsp-indexer/react` and `@lsp-indexer/next`, with infinite scroll and include narrowing.

slices.title: Build Validation & Docs
slices.demo: All 4 packages build clean, test app playground page exercises the 3 new hooks, docs pages updated.
slices.goal: All 4 packages build clean, test app playground page exercises the 3 new mutual follow hook families, docs pages updated with new hooks/actions/functions.

slices.title: Batch fetch across all packages
slices.demo: `useEncryptedAssetsBatch` returns encrypted assets for an array of `(address, contentId, revision)` tuples in one Hasura query. Verified by `pnpm build` across types, node, react, next.
slices.goal: `fetchEncryptedAssetsBatch` service function + React and Next.js hooks with full `EncryptedAssetInclude` type narrowing, all wired and building.

slices.title: Docs, changeset & build verification
slices.demo: Docs page documents batch API, changeset ready for minor release, full 5-package build passes including docs.
slices.goal: Document the batch encrypted asset API across all docs pages, create a changeset for minor release, and verify the full 5-package build including docs.

slices.title: Defensive hexToBool hardening
slices.demo: After this: pnpm --filter=@chillwhales/indexer build passes. All 3 hexToBool call sites use safeHexToBool. rg hexToBool shows only the safe wrapper definition and its usages.
slices.goal: Replace all raw hexToBool calls with a defensive safeHexToBool wrapper that returns false on invalid hex, fixing the production crash at block 7,137,664.
slices.success_criteria:
- `rg hexToBool packages/indexer/src/` shows only the `safeHexToBool` definition in utils and its 3 usages — no raw `hexToBool` imports from viem remain
- `pnpm --filter=@chillwhales/indexer build` exits 0 with no type errors
slices.proof_level: contract — build proves type-correctness of the replacement; runtime proof requires reprocessing block 7,137,664 which is out of scope
slices.integration_closure: Upstream: viem's `hexToBool` and `Hex` type from `packages/indexer/src/utils/index.ts`. New wiring: `safeHexToBool` replaces direct `hexToBool` at 3 call sites. Nothing else remains — the milestone is complete once this slice ships.
slices.observability_impact: None — the wrapper silently returns false on invalid hex, matching the existing pattern of safeHexToNumber/safeBigInt. No new logging or metrics.

tasks.title: Create changeset and verify full 5-package build
Add safeHexToBool wrapper and replace all raw hexToBool call sites to fix production crash on invalid Multicall3 return data
tasks.one_liner: Add safeHexToBool wrapper and replace all raw hexToBool call sites to fix production crash on invalid Multicall3 return data
tasks.narrative: Created safeHexToBool(hex: Hex): boolean in utils/index.ts following the existing safe wrapper pattern. The wrapper catches any error from viem's hexToBool and returns false. # T01: Add safeHexToBool wrapper and replace all raw hexToBool call sites to fix production crash on invalid Multicall3 return data

**Add safeHexToBool wrapper and replace all raw hexToBool call sites to fix production crash on invalid Multicall3 return data**

## What Happened

Created safeHexToBool(hex: Hex): boolean in utils/index.ts following the existing safe wrapper pattern. The wrapper catches any error from viem's hexToBool and returns false. Updated both handler call sites (orbsClaimed.handler.ts and chillClaimed.handler.ts) to use the safe wrapper. The task plan listed 3 call sites but only 2 existed — verification.ts does not use hexToBool.

## Verification

Build passes clean: pnpm --filter=@chillwhales/indexer build exits 0. grep confirms no raw hexToBool calls remain outside the wrapper definition in utils/index.ts.

## Verification Evidence

| # | Command | Exit Code | Verdict | Duration |
|---|---------|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | `pnpm --filter=@chillwhales/indexer build` | 0 | ✅ pass | 3200ms |
| 2 | `rg hexToBool packages/indexer/src/ | grep -v safeHexToBool (only utils lines)` | 0 | ✅ pass | 100ms |


## Deviations

Task plan listed verification.ts as a call site but it doesn't contain hexToBool. Only 2 call sites existed, not 3.

## Known Issues

None.

## Files Created/Modified

- `packages/indexer/src/utils/index.ts`
- `packages/indexer/src/handlers/chillwhales/orbsClaimed.handler.ts`
- `packages/indexer/src/handlers/chillwhales/chillClaimed.handler.ts`
tasks.description:
Create `safeHexToBool(hex: Hex): boolean` in `packages/indexer/src/utils/index.ts` that wraps viem's `hexToBool` in try-catch, returning `false` on any error. Then replace the raw `hexToBool` call at each of the 3 call sites:

1. `packages/indexer/src/core/verification.ts` line 233
2. `packages/indexer/src/handlers/chillwhales/orbsClaimed.handler.ts` line 177
3. `packages/indexer/src/handlers/chillwhales/chillClaimed.handler.ts` line 174

At each call site, replace `hexToBool(...)` with `safeHexToBool(...)`, add the import from `@/utils`, and remove `hexToBool` from the viem import. Prettier with `prettier-plugin-organize-imports` will auto-sort imports — don't manually arrange them.

The utils file already imports `Hex` and `isHex` from viem but needs `hexToBool` added to that import for the wrapper implementation.

Existing safe wrapper precedent in the same file: `safeBigInt`, `safeHexToNumber`, `safeJsonStringify`.

decisions.decision: How to compute follower set intersections
decisions.choice: Hasura nested `followed`/`followedBy` relationship filters on `universal_profile` query
decisions.rationale: Server-side SQL joins, no client-side intersection needed. Schema verified to support this.

decisions.decision: Return type for mutual follow hooks
decisions.rationale: The consumer wants to know *who* the mutual connections are, not the follow relationship metadata

decisions.choice: Query `universal_profile` table (not `follower` table) with nested relationship filters
decisions.rationale: The result is profiles, not follow records. Reuses existing profile parser and include machinery.

decisions.decision: How mutual follow service functions build where-clauses
decisions.choice: Call execute() directly with composed _and where-clauses instead of using fetchProfiles
decisions.rationale: fetchProfiles builds its own where from ProfileFilter, but mutual follow queries need two simultaneous followedBy/followed relationship conditions that can't be expressed through ProfileFilter. Direct execute() with Universal_Profile_Bool_Exp gives full control.

decisions.decision: Whether mutual follow hook factories should default params to empty object
decisions.choice: Mandatory addressA/addressB (or myAddress/targetAddress) params with no default = {}
decisions.rationale: Unlike profile queries which can be called without filters, mutual follow queries always require two addresses to compute an intersection — calling without them is nonsensical. Mandatory params prevent accidental misuse.

decisions.decision: How to batch-fetch encrypted assets by tuple
decisions.choice: Separate `fetchEncryptedAssetsBatch` using `_or`/`_and` Hasura where-clauses, not an extension of `fetchEncryptedAssets`
decisions.rationale: Input shape (tuple array vs single filter) and query shape (_or/_and exact tuples vs single _ilike filter) are fundamentally different. Matches precedent: fetchIsFollowingBatch is separate from fetchFollowers.

decisions.decision: Address comparison in batch tuple queries
decisions.choice: `_ilike` for address, `_eq` for contentId and revision
decisions.rationale: Non-checksummed address is exactly the same as checksummed — case-insensitive comparison required. contentId and revision are exact-match fields.

decisions.decision: Whether batch hook factory should use createUseList or direct useQuery
decisions.choice: Direct useQuery with manual 3-overload signatures, not createUseList
decisions.rationale: Batch results have no totalCount or pagination — createUseList adds unnecessary pagination machinery. Direct useQuery with enabled guard on tuples.length and stable EMPTY array is simpler and matches the createUseIsFollowingBatch precedent.

decisions.decision: How to handle non-boolean hex values from supportsInterface multicall responses
decisions.choice: Create a `safeHexToBool` utility that wraps viem's `hexToBool` in try-catch, returning `false` for any `InvalidHexBooleanError`. Place it in `packages/indexer/src/utils/index.ts`.
decisions.rationale: viem's hexToBool is strict (only 0x0/0x1). Rogue contracts can return arbitrary data for supportsInterface. Wrapping in try-catch is the simplest defensive fix — any non-boolean response means the contract doesn't properly implement the interface, so false is correct.

verification_evidence.command: rg hexToBool packages/indexer/src/ | grep -v safeHexToBool (only utils lines)
